items.title: Lock final copy for all four Track Record zones
items.description: Zone content is at ~80%. Need to finalize headlines, descriptions, and verify all metrics. Zone 1 (Scale) headline locked: 'Scale most PMs never touch.' Zone 2 description updated to use 'end-to-end user journey' instead of 'digital experience.' Remaining: final wordsmithing pass on all four zones.

items.title: Generate custom icon set for Track Record infographic
items.description: The mockup uses emoji placeholders. Need 12 minimal flat icons (scale arrow, shield/auth, payment card, AI brain, mobile device, marketplace, engagement bell, code brackets, lightning bolt, flask, document, compass). Recommended: Midjourney or Ideogram, single color (#f0e6d3), geometric, 48px weight. Claude-generated SVGs will look too sterile.

items.title: Build production Track Record page (Pass 1: structure + real content)
items.description: Integrate the four-zone infographic into career-highlights.html shell (keep nav, footer, theme toggle, Fenix widget). Replace all content area. Use real copy from locked content. Placeholder icons until custom assets arrive.

items.title: Rename Career Highlights to Track Record across site navigation
items.description: Update nav labels on all pages, page title, meta description, and any cross-references. Decide on URL strategy: keep career-highlights.html with canonical or rename to track-record.html with redirect.

items.title: Design the 'second act' — curated depth flow after Track Record
items.description: After someone is impressed by the Track Record page, how do they self-identify and receive curated initiative cards through their lens? Options: persona-picker-style flow, Fenix-served depth, or filtered card view. Needs a strategy session.

items.title: Visual polish pass on Track Record (Pass 2: icons + path + animation)
items.description: After custom icons arrive and Pass 1 is live, refine spacing, animation timing, responsive breakpoints, light-theme treatment, and potentially hand-designed SVG path shape (Excalidraw or Figma).
